title: Push notification delivery is best-effort
description: Push messages can be delayed, deduplicated, collapsed, or dropped by the OS and platform, so they must not be the only source of truth.
body: Use when designing features that depend on push notifications. Delivery via APNs/FCM is best-effort: messages may be throttled, collapsed by collapse-key, delayed in low-power states, or dropped if the device is offline too long, and tokens rotate. Do not treat a push as guaranteed or as the sole trigger for critical state. Actions: reconcile state on app foreground via a fetch, handle token refresh, and make handlers idempotent. Validation signals include a test where a dropped push still resolves correctly on next foreground sync. Risk boundary: relying on push for security-critical or billing actions risks missed events; always provide a server-reconciled fallback.
